notes: Single (modelId, providerId) primary pair pinned for every stage of the agentic loop. At runtime itotori sends provider.order=[providerId] + provider.allow_fallbacks=true + provider.zdr=true + provider.data_collection=deny. OpenRouter may report a ZDR-allow-list provider different from the requested primary after an HTTP 429; itotori records the provider actually served as a routing outcome, without an application retry or failover (ITOTORI-241; UTSUSHI-231 recorded DigitalOcean when Fireworks was requested). The superseded app-level alternateProviders[] + failoverPredicate chain (ITOTORI-238/239/240) was REMOVED: the app must not double-handle a 429 OpenRouter already resolved. If every ZDR-allow-list provider is at quota, OpenRouter returns the terminal error and the run surfaces it verbatim as a ModelProviderError; there is no app-level retry layered on top. Runtime evidence is derived by EXECUTING the patched bytes: the LLM's real translated draft is patched into Seen.txt (SJIS-bracket-wrapped so the KAIFUU-191 lexer classifies it as a Textout run), the real utsushi-reallive replay decodes the patched Textout, and the harness asserts the engine's OBSERVED TextLine bodies reflect the real translated text (no planted sentinel substring). Per-stage zdr / fallbackModels / seed / maxPriceUsd are intentionally omitted: the parser fills in canonical defaults. Each leaf explicitly declares maximumBillableCostUsd=$0.50, the operator-approved hard reservation ceiling for one paid physical invocation; maxPriceUsd is never substituted for it. Operator-level zdr downgrades require OPENROUTER_ZDR_DOWNGRADE; no file-level escape hatch.
